case_title: MINDY FREILE VS. TIM LINCECUM et al

docket_entries.description: OBJECTION TO DEFT'S MOTION TO QUASH SERVICE OF SUMMONS FILED BY PLAINTIFF FREILE, MINDY
NOTICE OF ENTRY OF ORDER/NOTICE OF RULING FILED DENYING DEFT'S MOTION TO QUASH SERVICE OF SUMMONS FILED BY PLAINTIFF FREILE, MINDY

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT TIM LINCECUM'S MOTION TO QUASH SERVICE OF SUMMONS. A R G U E D; THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING: GRANTED. DEFENDANT WAS NOT PROPERLY SERVED. MOREOVER, THE PRIOR ORDER WAS NOT INTENDED TO PRECLUDE THE RE-FILING OF THE MOTION TO QUASH ON PROPER NOTICE. JUDGE KAHN'S CLEAR RECOLLECTION IS THAT, AT THE TIME HE SIGNED THE PRIOR ORDER, COUNSEL FOR PLAINTIFF ACKNOWLEDGED THIS VERY POINT AND THUS THE POSITIONS TAKEN IN THE OPPOSITION PAPERS LACK MERIT. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN, REPORTER: TEANNA WARD, CSR # 11918

DISCOVERY 302, PLAINTIFF MINDY FREILE'S MOTION FOR RECONSIDERATION OF THE COURT'S JULY 26, 2013 DISCOVERY ORDER IS CONTINUED FROM AUG-27-2013 TO SEP-18-2013 AT 9:30 A.M. IN DEPT. 302. JUDGE PRO TEM: JAMES P. WATSON; CLERK L. FONG; MATTER NOT REPORTED. (302/JPT)
POS OF REQUEST FOR JUDICIAL NOTICE IN SUPPORT OF MOTION FOR TERMINATING SANCTIONS, OR IN THE ALTERNATIVE, ISSUE SANCTIONS; NOTICE OF MOTION FOR TERMINATING SANCTIONS FILED BY DEFENDANT LINCECUM, TIM

UNLAWFUL DETAINER MOTION 501, DEFENDANT TIM LINCECUM'S MOTION FOR TERMINATING SANCTIONS, OR, IN THE ALTERNATIVE, ISSUE SANCTIONS BASED ON PLAINTIFF'S FAILURE TO COMPLY WITH THIS COURT'S JULY 26, 2013 DISCOVERY ORDER IS DENIED IN PART AND GRANTED IN PART. MOTION IS DENIED AS TO TERMINATING SANCTIONS, AND GRANTED AS TO ISSUE SANCTIONS. NO OPPOSITION FILED. PLAINTIFF'S OBJECTIONS ARE OVERRULED. SFLR 8.10 PROVIDES IN RELEVANT PART THAT "BREACH OF LEASE" MATTERS ARE HEARD IN DEPARTMENT 501 "INCLUDING DISCOVERY NOT HANDLED VIA PRO TEMPORE SYSTEM," I.E. THE MOTION WAS PROPERLY CALENDARED FOR DEPARTMENT 302 AND TRANSFERRED TO DEPARTMENT 501 WHEN PARTIES FAILED TO STIPULATE TO PRO TEMPORE JUDGE HEARING THE MATTER. PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE ORDER APPROVED AS TO FORM. JUDGE RONALD EVANS QUIDACHAY, CLERK: M. OLOPERNES, NOT REPORTED

Plaintiff/Cross-Defendant Freile'S Motion For Reconsideration Of The Court'S July 26, 2013, Discovery Order
Off Calendar Sep-26-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Jonathan Bornstein, Esq. of Bornstein & Bornstein, (415-409-7611), for Mindy Freile, Plaintiff and moving party.
Peter M. Bransten, Esq. of Landau Gottfried & Berger LLP, (415-956-1630), for Tim Lincecum, Defendant and opposing party.

ARGUED. Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral arguments adopts the tentative ruling as follows: Plaintiff/Cross-Defendant Freile's Motion For Reconsideration Of The Court's July 26, 2013, Discovery Order is DENIED. Moving party failed to sustain its burden under CCP 1008. Sanctions granted the amount of $3,150 to be paid to Defendant's counsel within 5 days of notice of entry of order. Order is signed in open court. Judge Ronald Evans Quidachay, Clerk: M. Olopernes, Not reported (D501)

Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn For Terminating Sanctions, Or, In The Alternative, Issue Sanctions Based On Pltf'S Failure To Comply With This Court'S Jul 26, 2013 Discovery Order
Off Calendar Sep-26-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Jonathan Bornstein, Esq. of Bornstein & Bornstein, (415-409-7611), for Mindy Freile, Plaintiff and moving party.
Peter M. Bransten, Esq. of Landau Gottfried & Berger LLP, (415-956-1630), for Tim Lincecum, Defendant and opposing party.

ARGUED. Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral arguments adopts the tentative ruling as follows: DEFENDANT TIM LINCECUM's Motion For Terminating Sanctions, Or, In The Alternative, Issue Sanctions Based On Plaintiff's Failure To Comply With This Court's July 26, 2013 Discovery Order is DENIED in part and GRANTED in part. Motion is DENIED as to Terminating Sanctions, and Granted as to Issue sanctions. No opposition filed. Plaintiff's objections are overruled. SFLR 8.10 provides in relevant part that "breach of lease" matters are heard in Department 501 "including discovery not handled via pro tempore system," i.e. the motion was properly calendared for Department 302 and transferred to Department 501 when parties failed to stipulate to pro tempore judge hearing the matter. Prevailing party to prepare order approved as to form. Judge Ronald Evans Quidachay, Clerk: M. Olopernes, Not reported (D501)

.

Notice Of Motion And Motion For An Order 1) To Have Requests For Admissions Deemed Admitted; 2) Compelling Responses To Requests Fro Production Of Documents And 2) Compelling Responses To Form And Specially Prepared Interrogatories;
Off Calendar Jul-26-2013 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:
Appearance(s) on FRI-JUL-26-2013:
Peter M. Bransten, Esq. of Landau Gottfried & Berger LLP [415.956.1630] appearing for TIM LINCECUM, defendant / moving party;
No appearance by or on behalf of plaintiff.

The Court adopts its tentative ruling re Cross Complainant TIM LINCECUM'S Motion for an Order 1) to Have Requests for Admissions Deemed Admitted; 2) Compelling Responses to Requests for Production of Documents and 2) Compelling Responses to Form and Specially Prepared Interrogatories: PLAINTIFF'S PROCEDURAL OBJECTION IS DENIED. THE CORRECTION OF THE TIME OF THE HEARING BY ONE-HALF HOUR DID NOT PREJUDICE PLAINTIFF AS IT DID NOT AFFECT THE DUE DATE OF PLAINTIFF'S OPPOSITION. FURTHER, THE MATTER WOULD NOT PROPERLY BE SET IN DEPARTMENT 501 AS IT INVOLVES DISCOVERY HANDLED BY THE PRO TEMPORE SYSTEM (SEE LOCAL RULE 8.10). PLAINTIFF OFFERED NO SUBSTANTIVE OPPOSITION. THE TENTATIVE RULING IS TO GRANT DEFENDANT'S MOTION FOR AN ORDER (1) TO HAVE REQUESTS FOR ADMISSIONS DEEMED ADMITTED; (2) COMPELLING RESPONSES TO REQUESTS FOR PRODUCTION OF DOCUMENTS; (3) COMPELLING RESPONSES TO FORM INTERROGATORIES; (4) COMPELLING RESPONSES TO SPECIALLY PREPARED INTERROGATORIES; AND (5) FOR SANCTIONS AGAINST PLAINTIFF AND HER COUNSEL IN THE AMOUNT OF $5,625. RESPONSES SHALL BE SERVED AND SANCTIONS SHALL BE PAID WITHIN TEN DAYS OF SERVICE OF NOTICE OF THIS ORDER. Order signed in open court. Judge Pro Tem: Mark Schreiber; Clerk L. Fong; matter not reported. (302/JPT)

Off Calendar Jan-17-2012 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - ATTY. JONATHAN BORNSTEIN, TEL. NO. 409-7611 APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFF;
ATTY. PETER M. BRANSTEN OF THE LANDAU GOTTFRIED & BERGER LLP, TEL. NO. 956-1630 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANT AND MOVING PARTY.

RULING - A R G U E D; THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING: DEFENDANT TIM LINCECUM'S MOTION To Quash Service Of Summons IS Granted. Defendant was not properly served. Moreover, the prior order was not intended to preclude the re-filing of the motion to quash on proper notice. Judge Kahn's clear recollection is that, at the time he signed the prior order, counsel for plaintiff acknowledged this very point and thus the positions taken in the opposition papers lack merit. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Judge: Harold Kahn, Reporter: Teanna Ward, CSR # 11918 =302/HK
